session.user:
Search the codebase for how commands are marked as not requiring a repo. Check for: 1. Any decorator or marker called `no_repo_required` or similar 2. How the `pr list` or `pr view` commands are registered to work without a repo 3. How the objective group is defined and registered
Look in src/erk/cli/ for the command group definitions. Read docs/learned/ if relevant.
Report all findings including file paths and line numbers.

1→--- 2→title: Command Execution Strategies 3→read_when: 4→ - "executing commands in TUI" 5→ - "choosing between sync and streaming execution" 6→ - "implementing command runners" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "using subprocess.Popen in TUI code without stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL" 9→ warning: "Child processes inherit stdin from parent; in TUI context this creates deadlocks when child prompts for user input. Always set `stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL` for TUI subprocess calls." 10→last_audited: "2026-02-16 08:00 PT" 11→audit_result: clean 12→--- 13→ 14→# Command Execution Strategies 15→ 16→This guide covers the different strategies for executing commands in erk and when to use each approach. 17→ 18→## Two Strategies 19→ 20→### 1. Streaming Subprocess (Preferred) 21→ 22→**Pattern:** Execute command in subprocess, stream output back to UI in real-time 23→ 24→**Characteristics:** 25→ 26→- Real-time output visibility 27→- Non-blocking UI 28→- Requires repository context (`repo_root`) 29→- Uses background thread for I/O 30→- Cross-thread communication with `app.call_from_thread()`
... [125 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Perfect. Now let me check the NoRepoSentinel definition itself:
session.tool_result: Found 1 file packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/types.py
/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/types.py:class NoRepoSentinel:

1→"""Context types for erk and erk-kits. 2→ 3→This module provides the core data types used by ErkContext: 4→- RepoContext: Repository discovery result 5→- NoRepoSentinel: Sentinel for when not in a repository 6→- GlobalConfig: Global erk configuration 7→- LoadedConfig: Repository-level configuration 8→- InteractiveAgentConfig: Configuration for interactive agent launches (Claude or Codex) 9→- AgentBackend: Type for agent backend selection 10→- PermissionMode: Generic permission mode for both Claude and Codex 11→""" 12→ 13→from __future__ import annotations 14→ 15→from dataclasses import dataclass 16→from functools import cache 17→from pathlib import Path 18→from typing import Literal, Self 19→ 20→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import GitHubRepoId 21→ 22→# Agent backend types 23→AgentBackend = Literal["claude", "codex"] 24→ 25→# Claude CLI permission modes: 26→# - "default": Default mode with permission prompts 27→# - "acceptEdits": Accept edits without prompts (--permission-mode acceptEdits) 28→# - "plan": Plan mode for exploration and planning (--permission-mode plan) 29→# - "bypassPermissions": Bypass all permissions (--permission-mode bypassPermissions) 30→ClaudePermissionMode = Literal["default", "acceptEdits", "plan", "bypassPermissions"]
... [75 lines omitted] ...
51→ raise ValueError(f"Unknown permission_mode: {permission_mode}") 75→ raise ValueError(f"Unknown permission_mode: {permission_mode}") 99→ raise ValueError(f"Unknown permission_mode: {permission_mode}")

100→ 101→ 102→@dataclass(frozen=True) 103→class RepoContext: 104→ """Represents a git repo root and its managed worktrees directory. 105→ 106→ Attributes: 107→ root: The actual working tree root (where git commands run). 108→ For worktrees, this is the worktree directory. 109→ For main repos, this equals main_repo_root. 110→ repo_name: Name of the repository (derived from main_repo_root). 111→ repo_dir: Path to erk metadata directory (~/.erk/repos/<repo-name>). 112→ worktrees_dir: Path to worktrees directory (~/.erk/repos/<repo-name>/worktrees). 113→ main_repo_root: The main repository root (for consistent metadata paths). 114→ For worktrees, this is the parent repo's root directory. 115→ For main repos, this equals root. 116→ Defaults to root for backwards compatibility. 117→ github: GitHub repository identity, if available. 118→ """ 119→ 120→ root: Path 121→ repo_name: str 122→ repo_dir: Path # ~/.erk/repos/<repo-name> 123→ worktrees_dir: Path # ~/.erk/repos/<repo-name>/worktrees 124→ pool_json_path: Path # ~/.erk/repos/<repo-name>/pool.json 125→ main_repo_root: Path | None = None # Defaults to root for backwards compatibility 126→ github: GitHubRepoId | None = None # None if not a GitHub repo or no remote 127→ 128→ def __post_init__(self) -> None: 129→ """Set main_repo_root to root if not provided."""

250→ By default, shows only header information. Use --full to display 251→ the complete plan body. 252→ 253→ Examples: 254→ erk pr view 42 255→ erk pr view 42 --full 256→ erk pr view 42 --repo owner/repo 257→ """ 258→ if is_remote_mode(ctx, target_repo=target_repo): 259→ _pr_view_remote(ctx, identifier, full=full, target_repo=target_repo) 260→ else: 261→ _pr_view_local(ctx, identifier, full=full) 262→ 263→ 264→def _pr_view_remote( 265→ ctx: ErkContext, 266→ identifier: str | None, 267→ *, 268→ full: bool, 269→ target_repo: str | None, 270→) -> None: 271→ """Remote path for pr view using RemoteGitHub.""" 272→ if identifier is None: 273→ user_output( 274→ click.style("Error: ", fg="red") 275→ + "A plan identifier is required in remote mode (cannot infer from branch).\n" 276→ "Usage: erk pr view <number> --repo owner/repo" 277→ ) 278→ raise SystemExit(1) 279→

session.assistant:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report.
## Summary
Based on my thorough search of the codebase, here's how commands are marked as not requiring a repo and how the PR and objective groups are structured:
### 1. **No Repo Requirements Mechanism - Using `NoRepoSentinel` and Remote Mode Detection**
There is **no decorator** called `@no_repo_required`. Instead, erk uses a **sentinel-based pattern** with remote mode detection:
**Key File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/types.py` (lines 135-145)
```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class NoRepoSentinel: """Sentinel value indicating execution outside a git repository.
Used when commands run outside git repositories (e.g., before init, in non-git directories). Commands that require repo context can check for this sentinel and fail fast. """
message: str = "Not inside a git repository" ```
**How it works:** - `ctx.repo` is either a `RepoContext` or a `NoRepoSentinel` instance - Commands check `isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel)` to determine if they're in a repo
### 2. **How PR and Objective Commands Work Without a Repo**
Both `pr list` and `objective list` commands use the **`--repo owner/repo` option** combined with **remote mode detection** to work without a local repository.
**Key File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/repo_resolution.py` (lines 75-88)
```python def is_remote_mode(ctx: ErkContext, *, target_repo: str | None) -> bool: """Determine if command should use remote path.
Args: ctx: ErkContext target_repo: Optional --repo flag value
Returns: True if remote path should be used """ if target_repo is not None: return True return isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel) ```
**The `repo_option` decorator** (lines 90-96):
```python repo_option = click.option( "--repo", "target_repo", type=str, default=None, help="Target repo (owner/repo) for remote operation without local git clone", ) ```
### 3. **PR Group Definition and Registration**
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/__init__.py` (lines 25-48)
```python @click.group("pr") def pr_group() -> None: """Manage pull requests.""" pass
pr_group.add_command(pr_list, name="list") pr_group.add_command(pr_view, name="view") ```
The `pr_list` and `pr_view` commands are registered with the `@repo_option` decorator (line 13 and 17 of list_cmd.py and view_cmd.py respectively).
### 4. **Objective Group Definition and Registration**
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/objective/__init__.py` (lines 14-24)
```python @click.group("objective", cls=ErkCommandGroup, grouped=False) def objective_group() -> None: """Manage objectives (multi-PR coordination issues).""" pass
register_with_aliases(objective_group, check_objective) register_with_aliases(objective_group, close_objective) register_with_aliases(objective_group, plan_objective) register_with_aliases(objective_group, list_objectives) register_with_aliases(objective_group, view_objective) ```
### 5. **Remote Mode Implementation Examples**
**`pr list` command** - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py` (lines 541-596)
The command uses `is_remote_mode(ctx, target_repo=target_repo)` to choose between: - `_pr_list_remote()` - Uses `RemoteGitHub` API for operations without a local clone - `_pr_list_impl()` - Uses local `RealPlanDataProvider` with git context
**`objective list` command** - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/objective/list_cmd.py` (lines 138-191)
Uses `@repo_option` and calls: - `resolve_owner_repo(ctx, target_repo=target_repo)` to get owner/repo - `get_remote_github(ctx)` to get a `RemoteGitHub` instance - `remote.list_issues()` to fetch objectives from GitHub API
### 6. **Key Design Patterns**
**Pattern 1: Remote Resolution** ```python owner, repo_name = resolve_owner_repo(ctx, target_repo=target_repo) remote = get_remote_github(ctx) issues = remote.list_issues(owner=owner, repo=repo_name, ...) ```
**Pattern 2: Remote Mode Branching** ```python if is_remote_mode(ctx, target_repo=target_repo): _command_remote(ctx, ..., target_repo=target_repo) else: _command_local(ctx, ...) ```
**Pattern 3: Hook Context Support** In hooks (/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/hooks/decorators.py, lines 110-146), `NoRepoSentinel` is checked to gracefully handle commands outside repos:
```python def _extract_repo_root_from_click_context(args: tuple) -> Path | None: ... repo = obj.repo if isinstance(repo, NoRepoSentinel): return None ```
### 7. **Related Git Commits**
- `1b4bd5570`: "Add plan: Plan: Convert Objective Read Commands to @no_repo_required" - `83ba44ce1`: "Add no-repo infrastructure: sentinels and remote dispatch foundation (#8880)" - `0546aeb22`: "Implement RemoteGitHub read operations for PR commands (Phase 1 & 2) (#8916)"
All file paths are absolute paths in `/home/runner/work/erk/erk`.
